title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
full_text: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agen

The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agen

# The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials

## clean_text

Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agen

The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agen
